findings.reachability: Any unauthenticated or authenticated user who can load the admin page URL with a crafted ?tab= parameter. In practice this page is restricted to admins, so reachability is admin-only. The value is never echoed directly in THIS file, making it a hygiene/regression risk rather than an immediately exploitable XSS.
findings.justification: Line 17 assigns raw $_GET['tab'] to $active_tab without any sanitization (no sanitize_key, no esc_attr). In this template the variable is only consumed via ternary comparisons whose outputs are hard-coded string literals ('nav-tab-active' or ''), so no unsanitized value reaches the browser in this file. However the unescaped variable is passed to BSR_Templates_Helper::get_tab_template() (line 75) and included sub-templates, creating a regression surface if any sub-template ever echoes $active_tab directly.

detection_pattern.description: Catches WordPress admin pages that read a tab/page discriminator from $_GET without sanitizing it. Files matching the grep but not the anti_pattern pass a raw string into downstream template logic, creating an XSS regression surface whenever the value is echoed.
